rulings.Case Title: DALE RILEY, SR. VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P): AS REFLECTED ON et al

rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JANUARY 10, 2008 LINE 4. INTERVENOR UNIGARD INSURANCE COMPANY'S Motion For Leave Of Court To Intervene IS OFF CALENDAR. INSUFFICIENT NOTICE UNDER C.C.P. rulings.Case Title: DENISE CAICEDO VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JANUARY 10, 2008 LINE 5. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT: DENY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION. COURT TREATS SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION AS DANGEROUS CONDITION CAUSE OF ACTION PER GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 835. SEE C.C.P. SECTION 452. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RE WHETHER PATHWAY THAT PLAINTIFF FELL UPON IS A "TRAIL" WITHIN THE MEANING OF GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 831.4. =(302/PHA/PB)

rulings.Case Title: DENISE CAICEDO VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JANUARY 10, 2008 LINE 5. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. GRANT MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR MEDICAL NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION AS UNOPPOSED. =(302/PHA/PB)
